notes: Started Chapter 1 covering foundational security concepts including the CIA triad, authenticity, and nonrepudiation. Focused on understanding how governance and policy frameworks guide security programs.
Continued Chapter 1 and reviewed how security governance aligns with business objectives. Took notes on policies, standards, procedures, and guidelines and how they differ in practice.
Studied threat modeling concepts and supply chain risk management. Interesting to see how governance structures shape how organizations identify and manage risk.
Finished Chapter 1 and summarized core governance ideas and security principles. Created a quick reference for CIA triad concepts and policy hierarchy.
Started Chapter 2 covering personnel security concepts and risk management foundations. Took notes on how human factors and insider threats influence security programs.
Reviewed risk management frameworks and the role of organizational policies in managing security risk. Noted how structured frameworks help standardize security decision making.
Focused on social engineering threats and how attackers exploit human behavior. Added examples of phishing and pretexting attacks to my notes.
Studied the importance of security awareness, training, and education programs. Interesting to see how culture and training influence overall security posture.
Finished Chapter 2 and summarized key personnel security practices and risk management ideas. Flagged social engineering defenses as an area worth revisiting.
Started Chapter 3 on business continuity planning. Reviewed the purpose of BCP and how organizations prepare for disruptions and operational outages.
Studied the business impact analysis process and how organizations identify critical systems and acceptable downtime. Noted the importance of recovery objectives.
Continued Chapter 3 with continuity strategy development and recovery planning. Interesting to see how technical and business priorities must align.
Finished Chapter 3 and summarized the full BCP lifecycle including planning, implementation, and approval. Wrote out a quick checklist of BCP steps.
Started Chapter 4 reviewing legal systems and how laws influence cybersecurity practices. Took notes on criminal, civil, and administrative law categories.
Continued Chapter 4 with privacy laws, regulatory requirements, and compliance obligations. Interesting how different jurisdictions approach data protection.
Reviewed contracts, procurement considerations, and legal responsibilities around information security. Noted how vendor relationships affect compliance risk.
Worked through examples of compliance frameworks and regulatory oversight. Added notes comparing regulatory requirements to internal security policies.
Finished Chapter 4 and summarized major legal and regulatory concepts relevant to cybersecurity. Created a quick reference list of common compliance drivers.
Started Chapter 5 covering asset security and information classification. Reviewed how organizations identify and categorize sensitive data.
Studied asset ownership roles and responsibilities along with handling requirements. Interesting how classification decisions influence control selection.
Focused on data protection mechanisms and secure handling practices throughout the information lifecycle.
Finished Chapter 5 and summarized asset classification models and security baselines used to protect sensitive information.
Started Chapter 6 reviewing the foundations of cryptography. Took notes on encryption concepts and the role cryptography plays in protecting data.
Studied symmetric encryption algorithms and how secret keys are used to protect confidentiality. Noted common algorithm examples and their tradeoffs.
Reviewed cryptographic lifecycle concepts including key generation, distribution, rotation, and destruction.
Finished Chapter 6 and summarized symmetric cryptography concepts and where encryption fits into overall system security.
Started Chapter 7 focusing on asymmetric cryptography and public key infrastructure concepts.
Studied digital signatures, hashing algorithms, and the role they play in integrity and authentication.
Reviewed PKI components including certificate authorities, registration authorities, and certificate management.
Finished Chapter 7 and summarized how hybrid cryptography combines symmetric and asymmetric methods in real-world systems.
Started Chapter 8 covering secure system design principles and foundational security models.
Reviewed common security models and how they enforce confidentiality, integrity, and access control.
Studied system security capabilities and the role of controls in enforcing secure design principles.
Finished Chapter 8 and summarized secure architecture concepts and design techniques for protecting systems.
Started Chapter 9 reviewing common security vulnerabilities and threat categories.
Studied vulnerabilities across modern architectures including cloud, IoT, and containerized systems.
Reviewed common attack methods and countermeasures used to protect client, server, and distributed systems.
Finished Chapter 9 and summarized key vulnerability patterns and defensive strategies.
Reviewed Chapter 1 and completed the end-of-chapter practice questions. Focused on reinforcing governance concepts, the CIA triad, and how policies and standards support security programs.
Reviewed Chapter 2 and worked through the practice questions. Helped clarify personnel security concepts and how training and awareness programs reduce social engineering risk.
Reviewed Chapter 3 and completed the practice questions. Revisited business impact analysis concepts and the key steps in business continuity planning.
Reviewed Chapter 4 and completed the chapter questions. Focused on distinguishing legal frameworks, privacy regulations, and compliance responsibilities.
Reviewed Chapter 5 and completed the practice questions. Reinforced asset classification concepts and how handling requirements protect sensitive information.
Reviewed Chapter 6 and completed the end-of-chapter questions. Revisited symmetric cryptography concepts and key management fundamentals.
Reviewed Chapter 7 and completed the practice questions. Focused on PKI components, digital signatures, and how asymmetric cryptography supports authentication and integrity.
Reviewed Chapter 8 and worked through the practice questions. Reinforced secure design principles and how security models influence system architecture decisions.
Reviewed Chapter 9 and completed the practice questions. Focused on common vulnerability patterns and the countermeasures used to protect modern systems.
Reviewed concepts and definitions from Chapters 1–9 based on questions I answered incorrectly during the end-of-chapter practice reviews.

Started Chapter 10 on physical security requirements. Reviewed secure facility planning, critical path analysis, and how infrastructure dependencies like power, environmental controls, and networking support mission-critical systems. Also studied Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design (CPTED) principles and how environmental design combined with traditional controls can help deter and detect physical threats.
